correctness.notes: Theorem 1 sharpness/pasting step, label thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed: The reproduced conditional value set is I_x={int h_x d gamma: gamma in Gamma_x}. Convexity makes I_x an interval, but h_x=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)} is discontinuous on Q_1=Q_0, so compactness of Gamma_x does not imply the missing endpoint selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U attaining L_x and U_x. The note uses these selectors without an assumption ensuring endpoint attainment or closedness of I_x.; Theorem 2 finite-grid-to-continuous expansion, label thm:inference: The reproduced decomposition contains the omitted term A_n=sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-dotPhi_{rho,P}(sqrt(n)(hat eta_n-eta(P)))}. The note controls only the pointwise deterministic bias sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-Phi_rho(eta(P))}=sqrt(n)b_{K_n}=o(1), but gives no condition implying A_n=o_p(1).; Theorem 2 empirical indicator/sign-flip term, label thm:inference: The reproduced finite-grid objective changes by optimizer-weighted flips of 1{hat Q_1(u_l|x_j)>hat Q_0(u_m|x_j)}-1{Q_1(u_l|x_j)>Q_0(u_m|x_j)} on pairs with |Q_1(u_l|x_j)-Q_0(u_m|x_j)| less than the quantile error. This boundary-crossing contribution is absent from the expansion and is not shown to be o_p(n^{-1/2}).; Theorem 2 bootstrap replacement of Phi_{rho,K_n} by Phi_rho, label thm:inference: The reproduced bootstrap expansion contains B_n^*=sqrt(m_n){[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_{m,n}^*)-Phi_rho(hat eta_{m,n}^*)]-[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(hat eta_n)]}. The note drops B_n^* and uses only sqrt(m_n)b_{K_n}->0, but b_{K_n} is assumed only at eta(P), not uniformly over the random bootstrap neighborhood.; Theorem 2 stable-face bootstrap scale, label thm:inference: The proof uses stable dual faces stated for n^{-1/2}-neighborhoods, but the m_n-out-of-n bootstrap path from P has radius m_n^{-1/2}. Since m_n/n->0, this is larger than n^{-1/2}.

perItemFindings.label: k-sharp-dual-confirmed
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 1 sharpness/pasting step, label thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed: The reproduced conditional value set is I_x={int h_x d gamma: gamma in Gamma_x}. Convexity makes I_x an interval, but h_x=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)} is discontinuous on Q_1=Q_0, so compactness of Gamma_x does not imply the missing endpoint selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U attaining L_x and U_x. The note uses these selectors without an assumption ensuring endpoint attainment or closedness of I_x. → The proof establishes the outer bound Theta_I(P) subset [L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] and at most interpolation between attained values; equality with the closed sharp interval can fail or needs an added attainment/regularity hypothesis.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 2 finite-grid-to-continuous expansion, label thm:inference: The reproduced decomposition contains the omitted term A_n=sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-dotPhi_{rho,P}(sqrt(n)(hat eta_n-eta(P)))}. The note controls only the pointwise deterministic bias sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-Phi_rho(eta(P))}=sqrt(n)b_{K_n}=o(1), but gives no condition implying A_n=o_p(1). → Pointwise checkerboard bias at P does not justify applying the derivative of Phi_rho to the discontinuous finite-grid LP Phi_{rho,K_n}; the stated root-n limit can be wrong without a uniform local sieve-derivative/equicontinuity hypothesis.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 2 empirical indicator/sign-flip term, label thm:inference: The reproduced finite-grid objective changes by optimizer-weighted flips of 1{hat Q_1(u_l|x_j)>hat Q_0(u_m|x_j)}-1{Q_1(u_l|x_j)>Q_0(u_m|x_j)} on pairs with |Q_1(u_l|x_j)-Q_0(u_m|x_j)| less than the quantile error. This boundary-crossing contribution is absent from the expansion and is not shown to be o_p(n^{-1/2}). → Zero continuous-boundary mass under population optimizers gives consistency, not root-n negligibility of grid sign flips; the endpoint law needs a boundary-margin/rate condition or an explicit derivative term.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 bootstrap replacement of Phi_{rho,K_n} by Phi_rho, label thm:inference: The reproduced bootstrap expansion contains B_n^*=sqrt(m_n){[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_{m,n}^*)-Phi_rho(hat eta_{m,n}^*)]-[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(hat eta_n)]}. The note drops B_n^* and uses only sqrt(m_n)b_{K_n}->0, but b_{K_n} is assumed only at eta(P), not uniformly over the random bootstrap neighborhood. → The conditional bootstrap law is not established; a local uniform o(m_n^{-1/2}) checkerboard approximation is needed.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 stable-face bootstrap scale, label thm:inference: The proof uses stable dual faces stated for n^{-1/2}-neighborhoods, but the m_n-out-of-n bootstrap path from P has radius m_n^{-1/2}. Since m_n/n->0, this is larger than n^{-1/2}. → The asserted bootstrap derivative at the population face needs stability on m_n^{-1/2} neighborhoods or a fixed local neighborhood; the stated assumption does not supply it.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 1 / sharpness step for Θ_I(P)=[L_ρ(P),U_ρ(P)]
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof relies on endpoint-attaining measurable selectors for a strict-indicator transport objective, but the stated assumptions do not supply attainment or measurable selection.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 2 / root-n endpoint expansion
perItemFindings.one_line: The expansion drops the root-n local stochastic checkerboard residual, so grid-pair sign flips near Q1=Q0 can affect the claimed limit law.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 2 / bootstrap expansion
perItemFindings.one_line: The bootstrap proof replaces a shifted directional derivative by the population derivative without the needed continuity or uniformity condition, and lacks a bootstrap grid condition.

perItemFindings.label: Supporting theorem sieve-consistency / LP value continuity
perItemFindings.one_line: Uniform quantile consistency does not control optimizer mass on near-boundary indicator cells, so LP value convergence is not proved.

perItemFindings.label: Supporting theorem critical-set / majority-sign iff
perItemFindings.one_line: The majority-sign converse uses sharpness/attainment but states only sampling through band assumptions and introduces undefined singleton-rho envelopes.

review.verbatim_critique: Repair Theorem 1 by adding or proving the missing endpoint-attainment and measurable-selection ingredients for the strict benefit indicator h_x, or weaken the conclusion to closure or approximate sharpness. In the proof of Theorem 1, the paragraph beginning with endpoint values being attained is the load-bearing point: weak compactness alone is not enough for a discontinuous indicator objective. Repair Theorem 2 by carrying the stochastic checkerboard residual in the root-n expansion and adding conditions that make it negligible, including a near-boundary mass-control condition for grid sign flips. The bootstrap proof also needs an explicit condition ensuring uniform continuity of the directional derivative along the shifted path R_n+H_n^* and a bootstrap grid condition such as log K_n divided by m_n tending to zero. Revise the sieve-consistency supporting theorem by stating a mass-control condition over near-boundary grid cells for optimizers or near-optimizers, not just boundary zero mass for continuous optimizers. Revise the critical-set supporting theorem so the iff direction either explicitly invokes the repaired sharpness result and defines the singleton-rho envelopes L_x(rho;P), U_x(rho;P), or is weakened to the one-sided certification implications that follow from outer bounds alone.

correctness.notes: Theorem 1 / thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed sharpness and dual representation: The sharpness proof drops endpoint-attainment residuals such as U_x(P) - integral h_x dgamma_x^U. For h_x = 1{Q_1 > Q_0}, compactness does not by itself give an exact upper maximizer, a closed conditional value set I_x = [L_x(P), U_x(P)], or measurable endpoint selectors gamma_x^L,gamma_x^U. The proof also invokes constrained Kellerer no-gap for the strict-indicator/bounded-Borel cost without a stated regularity or duality hypothesis.; Theorem 2 / thm:inference root-n endpoint expansion: The expansion drops the stochastic checkerboard remainder sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n) - Phi_rho(hat eta_n)}. The stated b_{K_n}=o(n^{-1/2}) controls only the point eta(P); the local-uniform assumption in ass:finite-cell is only o(m_n^{-1/2}), weaker because m_n/n -> 0. Equivalently, grid indicator flips on B_{j,K_n}(O_p(n^{-1/2});P) can contribute sqrt(n) omega(O_p(n^{-1/2})) with no condition forcing this to vanish.; Theorem thm:critical-set majority-sign iff: The converse drops the gap between the outer envelope and the actual identified set: it assumes [L_R(P),U_R(P)] \ Theta_R(P) is empty. Re-deriving from the definitions gives only Theta_R(P) subset [L_R(P),U_R(P)]; L_R(P) <= 1/2 <= U_R(P) shows outer-envelope straddling, not feasible sign indeterminacy unless sharp closed-interval equality is imported.

The stated b_{K_n}=o(n^{-1/2}) controls only the point eta(P); the local-uniform assumption in ass:finite-cell is only o(m_n^{-1/2}), weaker because m_n/n -> 0. correctness.notes: Theorem 1 / \label{thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed}: endpoint attainment and integrated sharpness: The proof needs endpoint selectors \(x\mapsto\gamma_x^L,\gamma_x^U\) attaining \(\inf_{\Gamma_x}\int h_x\,d\gamma\) and \(\sup_{\Gamma_x}\int h_x\,d\gamma\). For the strict kernel \(h_x=\mathbf 1\{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)\}\), compactness of \(\Gamma_x\) does not give upper-endpoint attainment; \(h_x\) is an open-set indicator, so \(\gamma\mapsto\int h_x\,d\gamma\) is not continuous. The measurable-selector step also requires unstated standard-Borel/joint-measurability and nonempty optimizer-correspondence hypotheses.; Theorem 2 / \label{thm:inference}: root-n expansion of \(\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho\): The reproduced expansion contains the missing local sieve term \(\sqrt n\{(\Phi_{\rho,K_n}-\Phi_\rho)(\widehat\eta_n)\}\). Equivalently, strict-indicator sign flips on near-boundary grid pairs contribute a term of order \(\sqrt n\,\omega(C\sqrt{\log K_n/n})\), which is not assumed to be \(o(1)\). The proof only controls the pointwise bias \(\sqrt n\{\Phi_{\rho,K_n}(\eta(P))-\Phi_\rho(\eta(P))\}\).; Theorem 2 / \label{thm:inference}: bootstrap replacement of \(\Phi_{\rho,K_n}\) by \(\Phi_\rho\): The bootstrap expansion requires \(\sqrt{m_n}\{e_{K_n}(\widehat\eta^*_{m,n})-e_{K_n}(\widehat\eta_n)\}=o_p^*(1)\), where \(e_K=\Phi_{\rho,K}-\Phi_\rho\). The proof instead invokes \(\sqrt{m_n}b_{K_n}\to0\), but \(b_{K_n}\) is only the population checkerboard bias at \(\eta(P)\), not the local difference along the bootstrap path.; Supporting Theorem \label{thm:critical-set}: majority-sign iff outer envelope straddles \(1/2\): From definitions alone I reproduce only the one-sided certification \(L_R(P)>1/2\Rightarrow\theta>1/2\) for all feasible laws and \(U_R(P)<1/2\Rightarrow\theta<1/2\) for all feasible laws. The converse term is missing: \(L_R(P)\le1/2\le U_R(P)\) for an outer envelope does not by itself produce feasible latent laws on both sides of \(1/2\).

perItemFindings.label: k-sharp-dual-confirmed
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 1 / \label{thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed}: endpoint attainment and integrated sharpness: The proof needs endpoint selectors \(x\mapsto\gamma_x^L,\gamma_x^U\) attaining \(\inf_{\Gamma_x}\int h_x\,d\gamma\) and \(\sup_{\Gamma_x}\int h_x\,d\gamma\). For the strict kernel \(h_x=\mathbf 1\{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)\}\), compactness of \(\Gamma_x\) does not give upper-endpoint attainment; \(h_x\) is an open-set indicator, so \(\gamma\mapsto\int h_x\,d\gamma\) is not continuous. The measurable-selector step also requires unstated standard-Borel/joint-measurability and nonempty optimizer-correspondence hypotheses. → The closed sharp interval \([L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P)]\) is not proved under the stated assumptions; at most an outer bound or closure-sharp statement follows unless endpoint-attainment and measurable-selection hypotheses are added.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 2 / \label{thm:inference}: root-n expansion of \(\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho\): The reproduced expansion contains the missing local sieve term \(\sqrt n\{(\Phi_{\rho,K_n}-\Phi_\rho)(\widehat\eta_n)\}\). Equivalently, strict-indicator sign flips on near-boundary grid pairs contribute a term of order \(\sqrt n\,\omega(C\sqrt{\log K_n/n})\), which is not assumed to be \(o(1)\). The proof only controls the pointwise bias \(\sqrt n\{\Phi_{\rho,K_n}(\eta(P))-\Phi_\rho(\eta(P))\}\). → The claimed \(\sqrt n\) directional limit and hence the bootstrap target law do not follow; the theorem needs a root-n local checkerboard/sieve remainder condition, not just pointwise \(b_{K_n}=o(n^{-1/2})\) and bootstrap-scale local control.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / \label{thm:inference}: bootstrap replacement of \(\Phi_{\rho,K_n}\) by \(\Phi_\rho\): The bootstrap expansion requires \(\sqrt{m_n}\{e_{K_n}(\widehat\eta^*_{m,n})-e_{K_n}(\widehat\eta_n)\}=o_p^*(1)\), where \(e_K=\Phi_{\rho,K}-\Phi_\rho\). The proof instead invokes \(\sqrt{m_n}b_{K_n}\to0\), but \(b_{K_n}\) is only the population checkerboard bias at \(\eta(P)\), not the local difference along the bootstrap path. → The displayed bootstrap proof drops a pathwise approximation term. It can be repaired only by explicitly using or strengthening the local uniform sieve condition at the bootstrap path, and this still does not repair the missing root-n sample term above.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Supporting Theorem \label{thm:critical-set}: majority-sign iff outer envelope straddles \(1/2\): From definitions alone I reproduce only the one-sided certification \(L_R(P)>1/2\Rightarrow\theta>1/2\) for all feasible laws and \(U_R(P)<1/2\Rightarrow\theta<1/2\) for all feasible laws. The converse term is missing: \(L_R(P)\le1/2\le U_R(P)\) for an outer envelope does not by itself produce feasible latent laws on both sides of \(1/2\). → The iff sign-indeterminacy claim relies on the sharpness/attainment theorem. If Theorem 1 is weakened to outer or closure sharpness, this supporting theorem must also be weakened or explicitly assume sharpness.

proposed_assumption.label: ass:sharp-endpoint-attainment
proposed_assumption.statement: For P_X-almost every x there exist measurable endpoint selectors x↦γ_x^L,γ_x^U∈Γ_x attaining the lower and upper values of γ↦∫h_x dγ over Γ_x; equivalently the conditional value set I_x={∫h_x dγ:γ∈Γ_x} is a closed interval [L_x(P),U_x(P)]. The constraint correspondence x↦Γ_x is standard-Borel with nonempty, jointly measurable optimizer correspondence for the strict benefit indicator h_x=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)}, so that the integrated endpoint maps L_ρ(P)=∫L_x(P)dP_X, U_ρ(P)=∫U_x(P)dP_X are attained and the constrained Kellerer dual holds with no gap.
proposed_assumption.source: This is the attainment/closed-interval kernel for the discontinuous open-set benefit indicator; because the proof under the stated assumptions delivers only the outer bound (ass:regular explicitly disclaims sharpness/attainment), granting it recovers exactly the headline exact-sharpness direction, hence it is regime_defining and must be theorem-split (conditional theorem in Main theorems + unconditional sharpness left as an open Conjecture) rather than silently granted.

assumption_classifications.label: ass:sharp-endpoint-attainment

proposed_assumption.label: A-margin
proposed_assumption.statement: There exist constants c<∞, δ_0>0 and an exponent α≥1 such that, locally uniformly over a fixed neighborhood N of η(P) (containing the m_n^{-1/2}-bootstrap path, and a fortiori the n^{-1/2}-sampling path), for every x, every band-feasible coupling γ∈Γ_x, and every 0<δ≤δ_0, the near-crossing margin set satisfies γ({(u,v): |Q_1(u|x)−Q_0(v|x)|≤δ}) ≤ c·δ^α; in particular the exact crossing boundary {(u,v): Q_1(u|x)=Q_0(v|x)} is γ-null for every γ∈Γ_x.
proposed_assumption.source: Standard Tsybakov-type boundary-margin / no-tie regularity condition on conditional quantile crossings, routine in quantile-treatment-effect and partial-identification inference (cf. Chernozhukov–Hong–Tamer); it constrains only smoothness near the measure-zero crossing set and is orthogonal to the Spearman band [ρ_-,ρ_+] that defines the identification regime, so it is generic rather than the kernel claim.

proposed_restatement.statement: Theorem 1 (thm:k-sharp-dual-confirmed, closure-sharp primal envelope). Under Assumptions sampling–band, for each x define the conditional benefit-concordance values L_x(P):=inf_{γ∈Γ_x}∫ h_x dγ and U_x(P):=sup_{γ∈Γ_x}∫ h_x dγ over the moment-constrained copula set Γ_x, where h_x(u,v)=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)}, and let L_ρ(P)=∫ L_x dP_X, U_ρ(P)=∫ U_x dP_X. Then the sharp identified set is the CLOSURE of the integrated value set, Θ_I(P)=cl{ ∫ (∫ h_x dγ_x) dP_X(x) : (γ_x)_x an admissible measurable selection from (Γ_x)_x }, and Θ_I(P) ⊆ [L_ρ(P),U_ρ(P)]; the endpoints L_x(P),U_x(P) are infima/suprema that need not be attained and no measurable argmax selector is asserted. The dual functionals furnish a computable WEAK-duality outer bound (D_x(h_x) ≤ L_x(P) and U_x(P) ≤ -D_x(-h_x), with the corresponding integrated bounds on Θ_I(P)); the no-gap equalities L_x(P)=D_x(h_x), U_x(P)=-D_x(-h_x) are recorded as an open Conjecture in §8 (they require a separate constrained-transport strong-duality result for bounded Borel strict-indicator costs and are not claimed here). Supporting Theorem (thm:critical-set, outer-envelope form): the population majority benefit sign is certified (point-identified up to closure) whenever 1/2 ∉ [L_R(P),U_R(P)]; when L_R(P) ≤ 1/2 ≤ U_R(P) the sign is indeterminate at the outer-envelope level (the strong feasibility-witness iff is dropped).
proposed_restatement.rationale: The draft over-stated a partial-identification result: it claimed a CLOSED interval with attained endpoints/measurable selectors and primal=dual EQUALITY for a strict-indicator (lower-semicontinuous, not weakly continuous) kernel, where compactness gives neither attainment nor strong duality. correctness.notes: Proposition 1 / reverse strong-duality step: Weak duality reproduces D_x(g) <= inf_{gamma in Gamma_x(rho_-,rho_+)} int g dgamma with the displayed terms +lambda_- a_- - lambda_+ a_+ and shifted cost g+(lambda_+-lambda_-)uv. The reverse inequality is not reproduced: the proof asserts a bounded-Borel constrained-dependence duality for the Spearman moment interval int uv dgamma in [a_-,a_+], but Kellerer marginal duality alone does not add the uv-moment constraint, and the cited dependence-duality result is not shown to cover this exact bounded-Borel moment-constrained problem for both g=h_x and g=-h_x.; Theorem 2 / bootstrap directional expansion: Reproducing the bootstrap expansion gives a population increment around the drifting base eta0+m_n^{-1/2}R_n. From ordinary directional expansion at eta0 one first obtains dotPhi(R_n+H_n^*)-dotPhi(R_n), so the suppressed local-drift/nonadditivity term is A_n^*=dotPhi(R_n+H_n^*)-dotPhi(R_n)-dotPhi(H_n^*). The proof sets the increment directly equal to dotPhi(H_n^*)+o_p^*(1) using a shifted expansion, but Assumption regular gives differentiability only at eta0 and stable-dual does not explicitly state the required uniform shifted Hadamard expansion over conditionally tight H_n^*.
